p: Our application currently stores user sessions in the application server's memory. This approach has several limitations:

ul.li: Sessions are lost when the server restarts
Cannot scale horizontally - users get logged out when load balancer routes them to a different server
Memory pressure on application servers during peak traffic

p: With our planned migration to Kubernetes and the need to support 10x current traffic, we need a centralized session storage solution.
We will use Redis as our centralized session caching solution.

ac:structured-macro.ac:plain-text-body:
We will implement Redis for session management with the following configuration:
- Redis Cluster mode with 3 master nodes and 3 replicas - Session TTL of 24 hours with sliding expiration - TLS encryption for data in transit - Hosted on AWS ElastiCache for managed operations

tr.td: Lack of persistence and clustering makes it unsuitable for our HA requirements

ul.li: Not designed for this use case

tr.td: Performance not suitable for session lookups on every request
Cost projections showed 3x expense vs Redis at our traffic levels

ul.li: Sessions persist across server restarts and deployments
Horizontal scaling without session affinity requirements
Sub-millisecond session lookups improve response times
Session data accessible by all microservices
New failure mode - Redis unavailability affects all services
Need to update session serialization format
Monitoring dashboards need updates for Redis metrics
Redis memory usage - set up alerts at 70% and 85% capacity
Connection pool exhaustion - monitor active connections vs pool size
Network latency between app servers and Redis - should be <1ms

ol.li: Provision ElastiCache Redis cluster in staging environment
Implement session middleware with Redis backend
Add feature flag for gradual rollout
Migrate staging environment and run load tests
Decommission in-memory session code after 2-week bake period
